summary: A brief presentation by Dr. Jack M. Wilson detailing Steve Jobs' entrepreneurial journey, highlighting his core philosophies on customer experience, intuition, and resilience. It includes excerpts from Jobs' 2005 Stanford Commencement address and a personal anecdote illustrating his relentless drive and willingness to push engineers beyond perceived technical limits.

principles.statement: Start with the customer experience and work backward to the technology.
principles.rationale: Technology should serve the user's needs and experience, rather than building a technology first and trying to figure out how to sell it to the customer.
principles.source_quote: You‘ve got to start with the customer experience and work back toward the technology - not the other way around.

principles.statement: Trust that the dots will connect in your future.
principles.rationale: You cannot predict how your current actions and experiences will benefit you in the future, so you must rely on your gut, destiny, or intuition to guide you forward.
principles.source_quote: Again, you can’t connect the dots looking forward; you can only connect them looking backward. So you have to trust that the dots will somehow connect in your future.

principles.statement: You have to find what you love to keep going through hard times.
principles.rationale: When life hits you with severe setbacks, passion for your work is the only thing that provides the resilience needed to persevere.
principles.source_quote: I’m convinced that the only thing that kept me going was that I loved what I did. You’ve got to find what you love.

frameworks.name: The "Last Day" Test
frameworks.when_to_apply: When evaluating your current life path, career choices, or daily routine to ensure alignment with your true desires.
steps: Ask yourself each morning: 'If today were the last day of my life, would I want to do what I am about to do today?'
If the answer is 'No' for too many days in a row, recognize that you need to change something in your life.
frameworks.source_quote: ‘If today were the last day of my life, would I want to do what I am about to do today?’ And whenever the answer has been “No” for too many days in a row, I know I need to change something.

mental_models.name: Connecting the Dots
mental_models.description: A metaphor for understanding how seemingly unrelated life events and choices build upon each other. You can only see the pattern and value of these events in retrospect, requiring blind trust in the present.

mental_models.name: Awful Tasting Medicine
mental_models.description: A lens for viewing painful or devastating life events (like being fired from your own company) as necessary, curative interventions that ultimately benefit the 'patient' (yourself).
mental_models.example: Jobs viewed being fired from Apple as 'awful tasting medicine, but I guess the patient needed it.'

heuristics: Don't let the noise of other's opinions drown out your own inner voice.
Have the courage to follow your heart and intuition.

signature_quotes.text: Don’t let the noise of other’s opinions drown out your own inner voice. And most importantly, have the courage to follow your heart and intuition. They somehow already know what you truly want to become.
You‘ve got to start with the customer experience and work back toward the technology - not the other way around.
A lot of times, people don't know what they want until you show it to them.
Again, you can’t connect the dots looking forward; you can only connect them looking backward.
Sometimes life hits you in the head with a brick. Don’t lose faith.
signature_quotes.context: From his 2005 Stanford Commencement Address, discussing his firing from Apple.

signature_quotes.text: If today were the last day of my life, would I want to do what I am about to do today?
signature_quotes.context: From his 2005 Stanford Commencement Address, discussing his perspective on death and daily motivation.

anti_patterns.description: Starting with the technology and trying to figure out how to sell it.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It ignores the customer experience, which should be the starting point of product development.

anti_patterns.description: Relying entirely on customers to tell you what to build.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: People often lack the vision to know what they want until you actually show it to them.

anti_patterns.description: Letting the opinions of others dictate your life choices.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It drowns out your own inner voice and intuition, which already know what you truly want to become.
